title: Ensure managed identities are configured

input_row.title: Ensure managed identities are configured
input_row.description: Managed identities from Microsoft Entra ID allow function apps to securely access other Azure services without the need to provision or rotate any secrets.
input_row.rationale: Using managed identities with function apps eliminates the need to store and manage credentials to access Azure resources.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Settings, click Identity. 4. Ensure that in the System assigned pane, the Status is set to On, and an Object (principal) ID is displayed, or that in the User assigned pane, a managed identity is listed. 5. Repeat steps 1-4 for each function app. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list function apps: az functionapp list For each function app, run the following command to get the identity setting: az functionapp show --resource-group --name --query "identity" Ensure that type is set to SystemAssigned, UserAssigned, or both. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 0da106f2-4ca3-48e8-bc85-c638fe6aea8f - Name: 'Function apps should use managed identity'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Settings, click Identity. 4. To add a system assigned managed identity: 1. In the System assigned pane, under Status, click On. 2. Click Save. 3. Click Yes. 5. To add a user assigned managed identity: 1. In the User assigned pane, click Add. 2. Use the filter box to search for a managed identity. 3. Select the identity. 4. Click Add. 6. Repeat steps 1-5 for each function app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each function app requiring remediation, run the following command to assign a system-assigned managed identity: az functionapp identity assign --resource-group --name Remediate from PowerShell For each function app requiring remediation, run the following command to assign a system-assigned managed identity: Update-AzFunctionApp -ResourceGroupName -Name -IdentityType SystemAssigned -Force
input_row.impact: Minor administrative overhead to configure and manage role assignments for managed identities.
input_row.default_value: Managed identities are disabled by default for function apps.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control can be verified using Azure APIs and Azure CLI commands, as detailed in the audit section. It does not require SSH/RDP for manual checks nor any human judgment, as the properties of managed identities can be queried directly via service commands and APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_function_app_managed_identity_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
1. Use Azure CLI to list all function apps:
   Command: `az functionapp list`
   - This command will retrieve all function apps in the specified subscription, including their resource group and name.

2. For each function app, use the following command to check the identity settings:
   Command: `az functionapp show --resource-group <resource_group_name> --name <function_app_name> --query "identity"`
   - Validate that the returned `type` field indicates "SystemAssigned", "UserAssigned", or both.

3. If using Azure SDK for Python, utilize the `FunctionManagementClient` to fetch the identity settings:
   - Call `function_management_client.function_apps.get_identity(resource_group_name, function_app_name)`.
   - Similar to the CLI, check that the returned identity type is "SystemAssigned", "UserAssigned", or both.

**Pass/Fail Criteria:**
- Pass: The function app's identity settings indicate that at least one managed identity (either system-assigned or user-assigned) is configured.
- Fail: The function app's identity settings indicate that no managed identities are assigned (type is either null or "None"). 

This structured approach ensures that every function app's compliance regarding managed identities can be verified efficiently and programmatically, adhering to the CIS benchmark recommendations.
